--- meshctl.py
+++ meshctl.py
@@ -232,7 +232,184 @@

-def validate_vault(data: dict, is_update: bool = False, original_vault: Optional[dict] = None) -> list:

+def validate_access(access: Any, field_path: str, errors: list) -> list:

+    """Validate spec.access authentication, permissions, and encryption."""

+    if access is None:

+        return errors

+    if not isinstance(access, dict):

+        errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.access", "invalid",

+                                    f"{field_path}.access must be an object"))

+        return errors

+

+    # Validate authentication

+    errors = validate_authentication(access.get("authentication"), f"{field_path}.access", errors)

+

+    # Validate permissions

+    errors = validate_permissions(access.get("permissions"), f"{field_path}.access", errors)

+

+    # Validate encryption

+    errors = validate_encryption(access.get("encryption"), f"{field_path}.access", errors)

+

+    return errors

+

+

+def validate_authentication(auth: Any, field_path: str, errors: list) -> list:

+    """Validate spec.access.authentication."""

+    if auth is None:

+        return errors

+    if not isinstance(auth, dict):

+        errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.authentication", "invalid",

+                                    f"{field_path}.authentication must be an object"))

+        return errors

+

+    enabled = auth.get("enabled")

+    digest_algorithm = auth.get("digestAlgorithm")

+    credential_ref = auth.get("credentialRef")

+

+    # When authentication is disabled

+    if enabled is False:

+        if digest_algorithm is not None:

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.authentication.digestAlgorithm", "forbidden",

+                                        "digestAlgorithm cannot be set when authentication is disabled"))

+        if credential_ref is not None:

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.credentialRef", "forbidden",

+                                        "credentialRef cannot be set when authentication is disabled"))

+        return errors

+

+    # Authentication is enabled (or not explicitly disabled)

+    valid_algorithms = ("SHA-256", "SHA-384", "SHA-512")

+    if digest_algorithm is not None:

+        if not isinstance(digest_algorithm, str):

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.authentication.digestAlgorithm", "invalid",

+                                        f"digestAlgorithm must be a string"))

+        elif digest_algorithm not in valid_algorithms:

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.authentication.digestAlgorithm", "invalid",

+                                        f"digestAlgorithm must be one of {valid_algorithms}"))

+

+    return errors

+

+

+def validate_permissions(perm: Any, field_path: str, errors: list) -> list:

+    """Validate spec.access.permissions."""

+    if perm is None:

+        return errors

+    if not isinstance(perm, dict):

+        errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.permissions", "invalid",

+                                    f"{field_path}.permissions must be an object"))

+        return errors

+

+    enabled = perm.get("enabled")

+    roles = perm.get("roles")

+

+    # When permissions are enabled, roles is required and must have at least one entry

+    if enabled is True:

+        if roles is None or not isinstance(roles, list) or len(roles) == 0:

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.permissions.roles", "required",

+                                        "roles is required when permissions are enabled"))

+        elif isinstance(roles, list):

+            # Validate each role

+            seen_names = set()

+            for i, role in enumerate(roles):

+                if not isinstance(role, dict):

+                    errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.roles[{i}]", "invalid",

+                                                f"role at index {i} must be an object"))

+                    continue

+

+                # Validate role name

+                name = role.get("name")

+                if name is None or (isinstance(name, str) and name.strip() == ""):

+                    errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.roles[{i}].name", "required",

+                                                f"role at index {i} must have a non-empty name"))

+                elif not isinstance(name, str):

+                    errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.roles[{i}].name", "invalid",

+                                                f"role name at index {i} must be a string"))

+                else:

+                    # Check for duplicate role names

+                    if name in seen_names:

+                        errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.roles", "duplicate",

+                                                    f"duplicate role name: '{name}'"))

+                    seen_names.add(name)

+

+                # Validate role permissions

+                permissions = role.get("permissions")

+                if permissions is None or not isinstance(permissions, list) or len(permissions) == 0:

+                    errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.roles[{i}].permissions", "required",

+                                                f"role at index {i} must have a non-empty permissions array"))

+                elif isinstance(permissions, list):

+                    for j, perm_item in enumerate(permissions):

+                        if not isinstance(perm_item, str):

+                            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.roles[{i}].permissions[{j}]", "invalid",

+                                                        f"permission at index {j} must be a string"))

+

+    return errors

+

+

+def validate_encryption(enc: Any, field_path: str, errors: list) -> list:

+    """Validate spec.access.encryption."""

+    if enc is None:

+        return errors

+    if not isinstance(enc, dict):

+        errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption", "invalid",

+                                    f"{field_path}.encryption must be an object"))

+        return errors

+

+    source = enc.get("source")

+    cert_ref = enc.get("certRef")

+    cert_service_ref = enc.get("certServiceRef")

+    client_mode = enc.get("clientMode")

+

+    # Validate source field

+    valid_sources = ("None", "Secret", "Service")

+    if source is not None:

+        if not isinstance(source, str):

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.source", "invalid",

+                                        f"source must be a string"))

+        elif source not in valid_sources:

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.source", "invalid",

+                                        f"source must be one of {valid_sources}"))

+

+    # Validate clientMode field

+    valid_client_modes = ("None", "Authenticate", "Validate")

+    if client_mode is not None:

+        if not isinstance(client_mode, str):

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.clientMode", "invalid",

+                                        f"clientMode must be a string"))

+        elif client_mode not in valid_client_modes:

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.clientMode", "invalid",

+                                        f"clientMode must be one of {valid_client_modes}"))

+

+    # Validate conditional requirements based on source

+    if source == "Secret":

+        if cert_ref is None or not isinstance(cert_ref, str) or cert_ref.strip() == "":

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.certRef", "required",

+                                        "certRef is required when source is 'Secret'"))

+        if cert_service_ref is not None:

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.certServiceRef", "forbidden",

+                                        "certServiceRef cannot be set when source is 'Secret'"))

+    elif source == "Service":

+        if cert_service_ref is None or not isinstance(cert_service_ref, str) or cert_service_ref.strip() == "":

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.certServiceRef", "required",

+                                        "certServiceRef is required when source is 'Service'"))

+        if cert_ref is not None:

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.certRef", "forbidden",

+                                        "certRef cannot be set when source is 'Service'"))

+    elif source == "None":

+        if cert_ref is not None:

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.certRef", "forbidden",

+                                        "certRef cannot be set when source is 'None'"))

+        if cert_service_ref is not None:

+            errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.certServiceRef", "forbidden",

+                                        "certServiceRef cannot be set when source is 'None'"))

+

+    # Validate clientMode with source = None combination

+    if source == "None" and client_mode not in (None, "None"):

+        errors.append(to_json_error(f"{field_path}.encryption.clientMode", "invalid",

+                                    "clientMode must be 'None' when source is 'None'"))

+

+    return errors

+

+

+def validate_mesh(data: dict, is_update: bool = False, original_mesh: Optional[dict] = None) -> list:

     errors = []

     if not isinstance(data, dict):

         errors.append(to_json_error("", "parse", "YAML must be a mapping"))

+    # instances: non-negative integer

+    instances = spec.get("instances")

+    if instances is not None and not isinstance(instances, int):
